summary: Backend testing complete for WhatsApp Connection Monitor. All 15 pytest tests passed (100%) covering proxy endpoints, monitors CRUD, events, settings persistence, internal event webhook (presence + client_state), and WebSocket (hello + broadcast on internal event).

minor.issue: No authentication/secret on the internal webhook — it is exposed publicly via /api ingress. Anyone can POST presence/client_state events and trigger broadcasts and emails. Consider adding a shared-secret header check between the Node sidecar and FastAPI.
Public endpoint with no auth that wipes the WA session and all monitors — could be abused. Consider gating or removing.
PUT does not validate that email_recipient is provided when email_enabled=true — a user can enable email with recipient null.

action_items: Optional: add shared-secret auth to /api/internal/event so only the local Node sidecar can post events.
Optional: enforce email_recipient required when email_enabled=true in PUT /api/settings.
critical_code_review_comments: /api/internal/event is exposed externally via /api ingress with no auth — should accept events only from the local node sidecar (shared secret header).
server.py uses deprecated FastAPI @app.on_event('shutdown') — should migrate to lifespan handler.
send_alert_email failures are logged but not surfaced anywhere; consider an in-app notification on email failure.
Monitor add_monitor stores added_at as ISO string but Monitor pydantic model declares datetime — mixed handling is correct now but fragile.
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_PHONE=33612345678 events; settings reset to defaults after persistence test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend WhatsApp client is in 'qr' state (not paired). POST /api/monitors correctly returns 409 'whatsapp client not ready'. To simulate presence events without pairing, POST to /api/internal/event with type='presence'. WebSocket at wss://<host>/api/ws sends a 'hello' on connect and broadcasts 'presence'/'client_state'/'monitor_added'/'monitor_removed' messages.
